after the comprehensive refactoring series.

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-3l7.2","title":"Project Command: Export to AGENTS.md","description":"# Feature: Project Command (Export to AGENTS.md)

## Purpose
Export playbook to agent-readable formats (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md).
This is how accumulated knowledge gets into the agent's context.

## Usage
```bash
cass-memory project --format agents.md --output ./AGENTS.md
cass-memory project --format claude.md --top 30
cass-memory project --category testing,debugging
```

## Output Formats

### AGENTS.md Format
```markdown
## Memory System: cass-memory

Before starting complex tasks, retrieve context:
\`\`\`bash
cass-memory context "<task>" --json
\`\`\`

### Top Rules by Category

#### Testing (8 rules)
- [b-abc123] For React hooks, test effects separately with renderHook
- [b-def456] Mock external APIs at the service layer, not in tests
- ...

#### Debugging (5 rules)
- [b-ghi789] Enable DEBUG=auth:* for authentication issues
- ...

### Memory Protocol
1. PRE-FLIGHT: Run `cass-memory context` before non-trivial tasks
2. REFERENCE: Cite bullet IDs when following rules
3. FEEDBACK: Mark bullets helpful/harmful as you work
```

### CLAUDE.md Format (Condensed)
```markdown
# Project Rules (via cass-memory)

Testing:
• Test React hooks with renderHook, effects separately [b-abc123]
• Mock APIs at service layer [b-def456]

Debugging:
• Use DEBUG=auth:* for auth issues [b-ghi789]
```

## Implementation
```typescript
async function projectCommand(flags: ProjectFlags): Promise<void> {
  const config = await loadConfig();
  const playbook = await loadMergedPlaybook(config);
  
  // Filter and sort bullets
  const bullets = playbook.bullets
    .filter(b => !b.deprecated && b.state !== "retired")
    .filter(b => !flags.category || flags.category.includes(b.category))
    .sort((a, b) => getEffectiveScore(b, config) - getEffectiveScore(a, config))
    .slice(0, flags.top || 50);
  
  // Group by category
  const byCategory = groupBy(bullets, b => b.category);
  
  // Generate output
  const content = flags.format === "agents.md"
    ? generateAgentsMd(byCategory, playbook.deprecatedPatterns)
    : generateClaudeMd(byCategory);
  
  if (flags.output) {
    await writeFile(flags.output, content);
    console.log(`Exported to ${flags.output}`);
  } else {
    console.log(content);
  }
}
```

## Category Sorting
Categories are sorted by total effective score of their bullets,
so the most impactful categories appear first.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:08.353916-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:08.353916-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-3l7.2","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-3l7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:08.354826-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-3l7.2","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:26.102059-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-3l7.2","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:26.171665-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-3l7.3","title":"Binary Compilation + Distribution","description":"# Feature: Binary Compilation + Distribution

## Purpose
Compile cass-memory to standalone binaries for easy distribution.
Users shouldn't need Bun installed to use the tool.

## Build Targets
- Linux x64: dist/cass-memory-linux-x64
- macOS ARM64: dist/cass-memory-darwin-arm64
- macOS x64: dist/cass-memory-darwin-x64
- Windows x64: dist/cass-memory-windows-x64.exe

## Build Commands
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --outfile dist/cass-memory",
    "build:all": "npm run build:linux && npm run build:macos-arm && npm run build:macos-x64 && npm run build:windows",
    "build:linux": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=bun-linux-x64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-linux-x64",
    "build:macos-arm": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=bun-darwin-arm64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-darwin-arm64",
    "build:macos-x64": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=bun-darwin-x64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-darwin-x64",
    "build:windows": "bun build src/cass-memory.ts --compile --target=bun-windows-x64 --outfile dist/cass-memory-windows-x64.exe"
  }
}
```

## Binary Size Considerations
- Base Bun runtime: ~50MB
- Application code: ~200KB
- Dependencies: ~5MB (mostly AI SDK)
- Total: ~55MB per binary

## Distribution Options
1. GitHub Releases (primary)
2. Homebrew formula (macOS)
3. npm/bun global install (for developers)

## Installation Instructions
```bash
# macOS (Homebrew)
brew install cass-memory

# Direct download
curl -L https://github.com/org/cass-memory/releases/latest/download/cass-memory-darwin-arm64 -o cass-memory
chmod +x cass-memory
sudo mv cass-memory /usr/local/bin/

# npm/bun
bun install -g cass-memory
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:09.629265-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:09.629265-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-3l7.3","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-3l7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:09.630419-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-3l7.3","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-5eh.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:25:26.253012-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-3l7.4","title":"Documentation: README.md + Examples","description":"# Feature: Documentation

## Purpose
Comprehensive documentation for users and contributors.

## Files to Create

### README.md
```markdown
# cass-memory

Agent-agnostic reflection and memory system for coding agents.
Implements the ACE (Agentic Context Engineering) framework.

## Quick Start

\`\`\`bash
# Install
brew install cass-memory  # or download binary

# Initialize
cass-memory init

# Get context before a task
cass-memory context "Fix authentication timeout" --json

# Mark rules as helpful/harmful
cass-memory mark b-abc123 --helpful
cass-memory mark b-def456 --harmful --reason "Caused test failures"

# Run reflection on recent sessions
cass-memory reflect --days 7

# Check playbook health
cass-memory stats
cass-memory doctor
\`\`\`

## How It Works

cass-memory implements three memory layers:

1. **Episodic Memory** (cass): Raw session logs from all agents
2. **Working Memory** (Diary): Structured session summaries
3. **Procedural Memory** (Playbook): Distilled rules with feedback tracking

## Configuration

\`\`\`bash
# Set your LLM provider
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
# or
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

# Configure
cass-memory config --provider anthropic --model claude-sonnet-4-20250514
\`\`\`

## Integration

Add to your AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md:
\`\`\`
cass-memory project --format agents.md >> AGENTS.md
\`\`\`

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| init | Initialize configuration |
| context | Get pre-task context |
| diary | Generate diary from session |
| reflect | Run reflection pipeline |
| mark | Record feedback |
| forget | Permanently block pattern |
| stats | Show playbook health |
| doctor | System health check |
| audit | Check for violations |
| project | Export to AGENTS.md |
\`\`\`

### Examples Directory
- examples/basic-workflow.md
- examples/team-setup.md
- examples/ci-integration.md

-{"id":"cass_memory_system-3pb","title":"[Playbook] updateBulletCounter - Increment helpful/harmful count","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function updateBulletCounter(
  playbook: Playbook,
  bulletId: string,
  type: "helpful" | "harmful"
): void
```

## Purpose
**DEPRECATED**: Legacy function to increment helpful/harmful counters without timestamp tracking.

## Deprecation Notice
This function is maintained for backward compatibility only. **New code should use recordFeedbackEvent()** instead.

## Why Deprecated?
- No timestamp tracking → cannot calculate confidence decay
- No event history → cannot analyze feedback patterns
- No session attribution → cannot trace feedback source
- Prevents effective confidence scoring

## Implementation
```typescript
const bullet = findBullet(playbook, bulletId);
if (!bullet) return;

if (type === "helpful") {
  bullet.helpfulCount++;
} else {
  bullet.harmfulCount++;
}
bullet.updatedAt = now();
```

## Migration Path
Replace:
```typescript
updateBulletCounter(playbook, bulletId, "helpful");
```

With:
```typescript
recordFeedbackEvent(playbook, bulletId, "helpful", {
  timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
  sessionId: currentSessionId,
  context: "user marked as helpful"
});
```

## Removal Timeline
- **Current**: Maintained for compatibility
- **Future**: Will be removed in schema v3
- **Action**: Migrate all callers to recordFeedbackEvent()

## Dependencies
- findBullet()

## Related Functions
- recordFeedbackEvent() - modern replacement
- calculateEffectiveScore() - requires events, not just counts","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:56.393319-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:25.058609-05:00"}

## Dependencies
- findBullet()

## Related Functions
- recordFeedbackEvent() - modern replacement
- calculateEffectiveScore() - requires events, not just counts","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:56.393319-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:02:37.352414-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:02:33.603063-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-3tt","title":"[Playbook] findBullet - Find bullet by ID","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function findBullet(playbook: Playbook, bulletId: string): PlaybookBullet | undefined
```

## Purpose
Find a bullet in the playbook by its unique ID. Simple lookup utility used by all mutation operations.

## Implementation
```typescript
return playbook.bullets.find(b => b.id === bulletId);
```

## Return Value
- **Found**: Returns the PlaybookBullet object
- **Not found**: Returns undefined

## Usage Scenarios
1. Before updating bullet fields
2. Before recording feedback events
3. Before deprecating a bullet
4. Before pinning/unpinning
5. Validation that bullet exists

## Implementation Notes
- Simple array.find() operation
- O(n) complexity - acceptable for typical playbook sizes
- No error throwing - returns undefined for not found
- Caller should handle undefined case

## Common Pattern
```typescript
const bullet = findBullet(playbook, bulletId);
if (!bullet) {
  throw new Error(`Bullet not found: ${bulletId}`);
}
// ... perform operation on bullet
```

## Dependencies
- None (pure function)

## Related Functions
- updateBulletCounter() - uses this to find bullet
- recordFeedbackEvent() - uses this to find bullet
- deprecateBullet() - uses this to find bullet
- pinBullet() - uses this to find bullet","notes":"Existing playbook helper implementations already present; no code changes made. Released reservation; feel free to take over.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:49.607871-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:57:45.657411-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:57:35.446769-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-3yb","title":"[Cass] cassExpand - Get context around a line","description":"Implement cassExpand() to get context around a specific line.

## Signature
```typescript
async function cassExpand(
  sessionPath: string, 
  lineNumber: number, 
  contextLines = 3,
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<string | null>
```

## CLI Command
`cass expand "{sessionPath}" -n {lineNumber} -C {contextLines} --json`

## Implementation
- Use execFile() to run expand command
- Return expanded context as string
- Return null on error

## Error Handling
- Return null if session not found
- Return null if line number out of range
- No retries (expand is deterministic)

## Parameters
- sessionPath: Full path to session file
- lineNumber: Target line (1-indexed)
- contextLines: Lines before/after target (default: 3)

## Output
Returns context window around target line:
```
35: Previous line 3
36: Previous line 2  
37: Previous line 1
38: >>> TARGET LINE <<<
39: Next line 1
40: Next line 2
41: Next line 3
```

## Usage
Used to get surrounding context for search hits:
```typescript
const hits = await cassSearch("bug fix");
for (const hit of hits) {
  const context = await cassExpand(hit.sessionPath, hit.lineNumber, 5);
  console.log(context);
}
```

## Exit Codes
- 0: Success
- 4: Session or line not found","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.936811-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:03.184794-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:44:41.305872-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-41k","title":"[Cass] cassCapabilities - Get cass feature support","description":"Implement cassCapabilities() to check what features cass CLI supports.

## Signature
```typescript
async function cassCapabilities(
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<Record<string, boolean>>
```

## CLI Command
`cass capabilities --json`

## Implementation
- Use execFile() to run capabilities command
- Parse JSON output
- Return feature availability map
- Return empty object on error (assume no features)

## Error Handling
- Return {} if command not supported (old cass version)
- Return {} on parse error
- Log at debug level

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "search": true,
  "timeline": true,
  "export": true,
  "expand": true,
  "semantic_search": false,
  "streaming": false
}
```

## Purpose
Version-dependent feature detection. Use to:
- Gracefully degrade on older cass versions
- Enable new features when available
- Show appropriate error messages

## Usage
```typescript
const caps = await cassCapabilities();

if (caps.semantic_search) {
  // Use new semantic search API
  await cassSemanticSearch(query);
} else {
  // Fallback to keyword search
  await cassSearch(query);
}

if (!caps.timeline) {
  console.error("Timeline feature requires cass >= 2.0");
  return;
}
```

## Exit Codes
- 0: Success
- 2: Command not supported (old version)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:03.681228-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:03.186423-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:45:13.342707-05:00"}
@@ -88,7 +88,7 @@
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-72k","title":"[Validator] runLLMValidation - Scientific validation via LLM","description":"Implement the runLLMValidation function - Scientific validation via LLM using historical evidence.

SIGNATURE:
```typescript
async function runLLMValidation(
  proposedRule: string,
  history: CassHit[],
  config: Config
): Promise<ValidationResult>
```

VALIDATION RESULT:
```typescript
interface ValidationResult {
  verdict: "VALID" | "INVALID" | "VALID_WITH_CAUTION";
  confidence: number;          // 0.0 to 1.0
  reason: string;              // LLM explanation
  evidence: string[];          // Citations from history
  refinedRule?: string;        // Optional improved wording
}
```

USES PROMPTS.validator TEMPLATE:
```
You are a scientific validator for agent playbook rules.

PROPOSED RULE:
{proposedRule}

HISTORICAL EVIDENCE:
{formattedEvidence}

TASK:
Analyze whether this rule is supported by the historical evidence.
Consider:
- Does the evidence show this pattern worked?
- Are there counter-examples (failures)?
- Is the rule too generic or too specific?
- Is the wording actionable?

RESPOND WITH:
- VALID: Strong evidence supports this rule
- INVALID: Evidence contradicts or doesn't support this rule
- VALID_WITH_CAUTION: Some support but concerns exist

Include:
1. Your verdict
2. Confidence score (0.0-1.0)
3. Reasoning based on evidence
4. Optional refined wording if the concept is valid but phrasing needs work
```

VALIDATION CRITERIA:
- Pattern appears in successful sessions
- Few or no counter-examples in failed sessions
- Rule is specific and actionable
- Rule is not overly generic
- Wording is clear and unambiguous

WHY THIS MATTERS:
- Provides deep analysis beyond simple counting
- Can identify nuanced patterns humans might miss
- Suggests improvements to rule wording
- Adds scientific rigor to rule acceptance

DEPENDENCIES:
- formatEvidenceForLLM() - Format cass hits for prompt
- PROMPTS.validator - Validation prompt template
- LLM API (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
- CassHit type

LOCATION:
- src/validator/llm-validation.ts","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:53.067317-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:53.07528-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-75lr","title":"[Docs] Installation instructions for all platforms","description":"Document installation methods for all platforms with clear, tested instructions.

Methods:
1. Pre-built binaries (recommended):
   - macOS (arm64): curl -L https://github.com/.../cass-memory-darwin-arm64 -o cass-memory && chmod +x cass-memory
   - macOS (x64): Same for darwin-x64
   - Linux (x64): curl for linux-x64
   - Windows: Download .exe from releases
   
2. From source:
   - git clone https://github.com/.../cass-memory.git
   - cd cass-memory
   - bun install
   - bun run build
   - Binary in dist/
   
3. Global install (npm/bun):
   - bun install -g cass-memory
   - OR: npm install -g cass-memory

Prerequisites: 
- cass CLI installed and configured
- API key for LLM provider (OpenAI/Anthropic)

Verification:
- cass-memory --version
- cass-memory doctor (health check)

Include troubleshooting for common install issues","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:26.553392-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:51:12.314275-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-79k5","title":"[Tracking] ProcessedLog class - Track processed sessions","description":"Class implementation:
```typescript
class ProcessedLog {
  private entries: Map<string, ProcessedEntry>;
  private logPath: string;

  constructor(logPath: string);
  async load(): Promise<void>;
  async save(): Promise<void>;
  isProcessed(sessionPath: string): boolean;
  markProcessed(entry: ProcessedEntry): void;
  getUnprocessedSessions(allSessions: string[]): string[];
}
```

Purpose: Tracks which sessions have been reflected on to avoid duplicate processing

Methods:
- load(): Read TSV file into entries map
- save(): Write entries map to TSV file (atomically)
- isProcessed(): Check if sessionPath exists in entries
- markProcessed(): Add new entry to map
- getUnprocessedSessions(): Filter out already-processed sessions

Used by `cm reflect` to identify new sessions needing reflection.
Separate logs per workspace or global scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:50:31.118371-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:18:09.46226-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-7g0","title":"[Playbook] deprecateBullet - Mark bullet as deprecated","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function deprecateBullet(
  playbook: Playbook,
  bulletId: string,
  reason: string,
  replacedBy?: string
): void
```

## Purpose
Mark a bullet as deprecated/retired. Used when rules become obsolete or are replaced by better rules.

## Implementation Steps
1. Find bullet by ID
2. Set state: "retired"
3. Set maturity: "deprecated"
4. Set deprecationReason to provided reason
5. Set replacedBy if provided (ID of replacement bullet)
6. Set updatedAt to now()

## Deprecation vs Deletion
**Never delete bullets**:
- Preserves history and context
- Allows rollback if needed
- Maintains source session references
- Enables learning from past mistakes

Deprecated bullets:
- Excluded from active context
- Shown in stats with deprecation reason
- Can be searched for historical analysis

## Common Deprecation Reasons
- "Superseded by more specific rule"
- "No longer applicable to current architecture"
- "Merged into {bulletId}"
- "Contradicted by proven evidence"
- "Too broad - split into multiple rules"

## Replacement Tracking
If replacedBy is provided:
- Creates chain: old → new
- Stats can show evolution
- Prevents re-adding deprecated patterns

## Usage Scenarios
1. **Manual deprecation**: User runs `forget` command
2. **Merge operation**: Old bullets point to merged result
3. **Auto-prune**: Harmful bullets beyond threshold
4. **Refactoring**: Rule split or restructuring

## Dependencies
- findBullet()

## Related Functions
- getActiveBullets() - filters out deprecated
- findMergeCandidates() - may create deprecations
- autoPrune() - deprecates harmful bullets","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:58.353949-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:01.293131-05:00"}

- findMergeCandidates() - may create deprecations
- autoPrune() - deprecates harmful bullets","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:58.353949-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:02:37.355037-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:02:33.601254-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-7hok","title":"[Critical] Privacy-First Cross-Agent - Opt-in with Clear Data Flow","description":"# Privacy-First Cross-Agent Enrichment

## The Problem
Current plan enables cross-agent learning by default. This means:
- Claude Code sessions inform Cursor behavior
- Personal coding patterns shared across tools
- No clear visibility into what's being shared

Users may not want this. Privacy-conscious users will reject the tool entirely.

## The Solution
Make cross-agent COMPLETELY opt-in with explicit consent:

### Default: Single-Agent Mode
```typescript
const DEFAULT_CONFIG = {
  crossAgent: {
    enabled: false,  // OFF by default
    consentGiven: false,
    consentDate: null,
    agents: []  // No agents enabled
  }
};
```

### Explicit Consent Flow:
```bash
$ cm init

Welcome to CASS Memory System!

Cross-Agent Learning (Optional):
CASS can learn from ALL your coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, etc.)
This means patterns from one tool improve others.

Enable cross-agent learning? [y/N]: n

OK, running in single-agent mode.
You can enable later with: cm config set crossAgent.enabled true
```

### Data Flow Visibility:
```bash
$ cm privacy status

Cross-Agent Learning: DISABLED
Data shared: None

If enabled, these agents would contribute:
  - Claude Code: 45 sessions
  - Cursor: 12 sessions
  - Codex: 3 sessions

To enable: cm config set crossAgent.enabled true
```

## Implementation Requirements
- [ ] Default crossAgent.enabled = false
- [ ] Consent prompt during init
- [ ] `cm privacy` command suite
- [ ] Per-agent enable/disable
- [ ] Audit logging of data flow

## Why This Is Critical
- Privacy is non-negotiable for many users
- Trust requires transparency
- Opt-in > Opt-out for sensitive features
- Single-agent mode must be fully functional","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:04.803971-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:53:14.716333-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-7my","title":"[Types] PlaybookBullet Schema Part 5 - Provenance and Verification","description":"## Overview
Define provenance tracking and verification system for PlaybookBullet. Answers: Where did this rule come from? Why does it exist? How can we verify it's still valid?

## Temporal Provenance

### createdAt: string
- Type: Required ISO 8601 timestamp
- Purpose: When rule was first added to playbook
- Usage: Calculate rule age / Audit rule creation timeline / Sort playbook by creation date
- Example: '2025-12-07T14:30:00Z'
- Immutable: Never changes after creation

### updatedAt: string
- Type: Required ISO 8601 timestamp
- Purpose: When rule was last modified (content, state, feedback)
- Usage: Detect stale rules (long time since update) / Track edit history / Trigger revalidation
- Example: '2025-12-07T16:45:00Z'
- Updates: On any field change (content, helpful marks, state transitions)

## Protection from Auto-Pruning

### pinned: boolean
- Type: Boolean flag (default: false)
- Purpose: Protect rule from automatic retirement/deletion
- Behavior: Immune to harmful threshold pruning / Immune to low-confidence cleanup / Immune to deduplication merging / Still participates in feedback and decay
- Use Cases: Critical security rules / Team mandates / Legal/compliance requirements
- Override: Manual deletion still possible

### pinnedReason: string | undefined
- Type: Optional explanation
- Purpose: Document WHY rule is protected
- Required: Should be set when pinned = true
- Examples: 'Security compliance requirement' / 'Team mandate from 2025-11-01 standup' / 'Critical bug prevention - see incident #1234'
- Display: Shown with pinned indicator in UI

## Source Tracking

### sourceSessions: string[]
- Type: Array of session file paths
- Purpose: Which cass sessions contributed to creating this rule
- Usage: Trace rule origins / Find supporting evidence / Link rules to historical work
- Population: Added by reflector from analyzed sessions / Added by manual creation with --session flag
- Example: ['/Users/dev/.cass/sessions/2025-12-07-auth-fix.jsonl', '/Users/dev/.cass/sessions/2025-12-05-token-validation.jsonl']

### sourceAgents: string[]
- Type: Array of agent identifiers
- Purpose: Which agents contributed to creating this rule
- Usage: Multi-agent collaboration tracking / Agent-specific rule analysis / Cross-agent pattern detection
- Values: 'claude', 'codex', 'cursor', 'human', etc.
- Example: ['claude', 'cursor'] (Rule emerged from Claude and Cursor sessions)

## Reasoning and Derivation

### reasoning: string | undefined
- Type: Optional free-form explanation
- Purpose: WHY this rule was created
- Source: Generated by reflector or provided manually
- Content: Problem it solves / Context of creation / Design rationale
- Example: 'Created after authentication bypass incident. Multiple sessions showed confusion about JWT validation. This rule prevents insecure token handling.'

### derivedFrom: object | undefined
- Type: Optional structured provenance
- Purpose: Detailed evidence trail from diary/sessions
- Structure: { diaryIds: string[], keyEvidence: string[] }
- Example: { diaryIds: ['diary-a3f8b2c1', 'diary-d9e4f7b2'], keyEvidence: ['Session showed JWT validation failure led to security bug', 'Team discussion confirmed need for centralized token validation'] }

## Automated Verification

### verification: object | undefined
- Type: Optional verification specification
- Purpose: Programmatic check that rule is still applicable
- Structure: { type: 'regex' | 'file_exists' | 'cass_query', pattern: string, mode: 'exists' | 'absent' }

Verification Types:

regex - Check if code pattern exists/absent in codebase
Example: { type: 'regex', pattern: 'TokenValidator', mode: 'exists' } (Fail if TokenValidator not found)

file_exists - Check if specific file/module exists
Example: { type: 'file_exists', pattern: 'lib/auth.ts', mode: 'exists' } (Fail if file doesn't exist)

cass_query - Check if cass search returns results
Example: { type: 'cass_query', pattern: 'vitest configuration', mode: 'exists' } (Fail if no vitest usage found)

Verification Workflow:
1. Periodic Checks: Run during 'cass memory validate --all'
2. On-Demand: Run 'cass memory verify <bulletId>'
3. Results: PASS (update lastValidatedAt) or FAIL (suggest deprecation)
4. Actions on Failure: Move to draft state / Add deprecation warning / Notify user

## Complete Example

{
  createdAt: '2025-11-15T10:00:00Z',
  updatedAt: '2025-12-07T14:30:00Z',
  pinned: true,
  pinnedReason: 'Critical security requirement per audit #2024-11',
  sourceSessions: ['/Users/dev/.cass/sessions/2025-11-15-auth-refactor.jsonl', '/Users/dev/.cass/sessions/2025-11-20-token-validation.jsonl'],
  sourceAgents: ['claude', 'cursor'],
  reasoning: 'After security audit revealed JWT validation gaps, consolidated token validation into single service to prevent bypass attacks.',
  derivedFrom: { diaryIds: ['diary-a3f8b2c1', 'diary-d9e4f7b2'], keyEvidence: ['Security audit found 3 instances of unsafe JWT handling', 'Team decision to centralize auth in TokenValidator service'] },
  verification: { type: 'file_exists', pattern: 'lib/auth/TokenValidator.ts', mode: 'exists' }
}

## Use Cases
- Security Compliance: Pin critical rules, verify dependencies, track audit trail
- Team Collaboration: Show agent contributions, link to discussions, explain decisions
- Maintenance: Detect outdated rules (verification fails), find stale rules (old updatedAt), protect important rules
- Debugging: Trace rule origins, find supporting evidence, understand reasoning

## Acceptance Criteria
- createdAt required timestamp field
- updatedAt required timestamp field
- pinned boolean field (default false)
- pinnedReason optional string field
- sourceSessions array field
- sourceAgents array field
- reasoning optional string field
- derivedFrom optional structured field
- verification optional structured field
- Verification runner implementation
- Auto-update updatedAt on changes
- Pin protection in prune logic
- Examples for all verification types
- JSDoc for all fields","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:01.583813-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:53:02.247027-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-7rz","title":"[Decay] checkForDemotion - Automatic maturity regression","description":"Automatically demote or deprecate bullets that accumulate harmful marks or whose scores decay into negative territory.

COMPLETE IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
/**
 * Check if bullet should be demoted or auto-deprecated.
 * Allows rules to 'fall' in maturity if they accumulate harmful marks.
 */
function checkForDemotion(bullet: PlaybookBullet): BulletMaturity | 'auto-deprecate' {
  const score = getEffectiveScore(bullet);

  // If score goes negative, consider demotion
  if (score < 0 && !bullet.pinned) {
    if (score < -3) {
      return 'auto-deprecate';  // Signals auto-deprecation
    }
    
    // Demote proven → established, established → candidate
    if (bullet.maturity === 'proven') return 'established';
    if (bullet.maturity === 'established') return 'candidate';
  }

  return bullet.maturity;
}
```

DEMOTION CRITERIA:

Effective score < 0 (negative):
- proven → established
- established → candidate
- Pinned bullets are PROTECTED from demotion

Effective score < -3 (severely negative):
- Any maturity → auto-deprecate
- Signals bullet should be moved to deprecated state
- Pinned bullets are PROTECTED

WHY DEMOTION MATTERS:
Rules can become toxic over time as codebase evolves. A rule that was once helpful might now be harmful. Demotion provides graceful degradation before full deprecation.

EXAMPLE SCENARIOS:

1. DEMOTE proven → established:
   - Was helpful 10 times last year (now decayed to ~2.5)
   - Marked harmful 1 time recently
   - Score = 2.5 - 4 = -1.5
   - Proven maturity multiplier = 1.5
   - Effective score = -1.5 × 1.5 = -2.25
   - Demoted to established

2. AUTO-DEPRECATE:
   - 3 recent harmful marks
   - 1 old helpful mark (decayed to 0.25)
   - Score = 0.25 - (4 × 3) = -11.75
   - Score < -3 → auto-deprecate
   - Bullet moved to deprecated state

3. PINNED PROTECTION:
   - Manually curated rule with pinned: true
   - Score = -5 (would normally auto-deprecate)
   - Returns current maturity (no change)
   - Human review required via forget command

INTEGRATION:
- Called by applyMaturityTransitions()
- Called after each curate cycle
- Returns 'auto-deprecate' string (not BulletMaturity type) to signal deprecation
- Pinned bullets bypass all automatic demotion

PROTECTION MECHANISM:
Pinned bullets cannot be auto-demoted or auto-deprecated. This allows humans to override the algorithm for critical rules. Use sparingly.

DEPENDENCIES:
- getEffectiveScore()
- PlaybookBullet.pinned field
- PlaybookBullet.maturity field

USED BY:
- applyMaturityTransitions()
- Auto-deprecation workflow","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:47.886037-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:44.305148-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-apt","title":"[Cass] cassSearch - Execute cass search with options","description":"Implement cassSearch() to execute cass search with comprehensive options.

## Signature
```typescript
interface CassSearchOptions {
  limit?: number;
  days?: number;
  agent?: string;
  workspace?: string;
  fields?: string;
  maxTokens?: number;
  highlight?: boolean;
  timeout?: number;
}

interface CassHit {
  sessionPath: string;
  lineNumber: number;
  content: string;
  timestamp?: string;
  score?: number;
}

async function cassSearch(
  query: string, 
  options: CassSearchOptions = {},
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<CassHit[]>
```

## CLI Command
`cass search "{query}" --robot [--limit N] [--days N] [--agent A] [--workspace W] [--fields F] [--max-tokens N] [--highlight] [--timeout N]`

## Implementation
- Build command with all provided options
- Use execFile() with maxBuffer: 50MB for large results
- Parse JSON output from --robot flag
- Return array of CassHit objects

## Error Handling
Check exit code and handle intelligently:
- Exit 0: Success, parse and return hits
- Exit 3: Index missing - rebuild index, then retry once
- Exit 10: Timeout - reduce limit by 50%, retry once
- Exit 4: Not found - return empty array
- Other errors: Log and return empty array

## Options Details
- limit: Max hits to return (default: 10)
- days: Only search last N days
- agent: Filter by agent name (e.g., "claude-code")
- workspace: Filter by workspace path
- fields: Comma-separated fields to search
- maxTokens: Truncate results to N tokens
- highlight: Include highlighting markers in results
- timeout: Command timeout in seconds

## Usage
```typescript
const hits = await cassSearch("bug fix", { 
  limit: 20, 
  days: 7, 
  agent: "claude-code" 
});
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:57.153732-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T19:02:10.078585-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T19:02:00.15937-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ar2","title":"[Curator] invertToAntiPattern - Convert harmful rule to pitfall","description":"Convert a harmful bullet into an anti-pattern (learning from mistakes).

Signature:
function invertToAntiPattern(bullet: PlaybookBullet): PlaybookBullet

KEY INNOVATION: Instead of deleting harmful rules, INVERT them to preserve learning.

Process:
1. Create new bullet with inverted semantics:
   - content: Prefix with 'AVOID: ' or 'DON\'T: '
   - Example: 'Use any type' → 'AVOID: Using any type without explicit reason'
   
2. Set bullet fields:
   - type: 'anti-pattern'
   - kind: 'anti_pattern'
   - category: same as original
   - maturity: 'candidate' (starts fresh)
   - state: 'active'
   
3. Preserve provenance:
   - source: copy from original
   - originalId: link back to harmful bullet
   - createdAt: current timestamp
   - derivedFrom: original bullet ID
   
4. Reset feedback (fresh start):
   - helpfulCount: 0
   - harmfulCount: 0
   - helpfulEvents: []
   - harmfulEvents: []
   
5. Return new anti-pattern bullet

Why inversion matters:
- Preserves WHAT NOT TO DO
- Prevents future developers from making same mistake
- Converts negative experience into positive guidance
- Maintains complete learning history

Example transformation:
Original (harmful): 'Always use any type for flexibility'
Anti-pattern: 'AVOID: Using any type without explicit reason. Prefer specific types for type safety.'

The anti-pattern must earn trust like any other candidate bullet.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:04.707658-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T17:14:47.213445-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-b5q","title":"[Security] compileExtraPatterns - User pattern compilation","description":"Implement compileExtraPatterns() to convert user pattern strings to RegExp objects.

Function signature:
```typescript
function compileExtraPatterns(patterns: string[]): RegExp[]
```

Implementation requirements:
1. Parse string patterns into RegExp objects
2. Add 'gi' flags automatically (global, case-insensitive)
3. Catch and warn on invalid regex syntax
4. Return only valid patterns (skip invalid ones)

Error handling:
- Log warning for invalid patterns: "[sanitize] Invalid regex pattern: <pattern>"
- Continue processing remaining patterns
- Never throw - return partial results

Example:
```typescript
const patterns = [
  "INTERNAL_[A-Z_]+_KEY",
  "[invalid(regex",  // syntax error
  "MY_SECRET_\\w+"
];
const compiled = compileExtraPatterns(patterns);
// Returns: [/INTERNAL_[A-Z_]+_KEY/gi, /MY_SECRET_\w+/gi]
// Logs: "[sanitize] Invalid regex pattern: [invalid(regex"
```

Called at startup to pre-compile user patterns from config.

Priority: 0 (Security Critical - P12)","notes":"Implemented compileExtraPatterns in src/sanitize.ts with gi flags, error handling, and warnings","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:51.253402-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:53:18.249516-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:52:23.874542-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-b96","title":"[Generator] generateSuggestedQueries - Follow-up search suggestions","description":"Generate human-readable cass search suggestions for follow-up investigation.

SIGNATURE:
function generateSuggestedQueries(task: string, keywords: string[]): string[]

PURPOSE:
Provide ready-to-run cass commands that the user can execute to dig deeper if the provided context isn't sufficient. Helps with iterative investigation.

OUTPUT:
Array of 3-5 formatted cass command strings

FORMAT:
'cass search "{query}" --days {days} [--agent {agent}]'

EXAMPLE:
Task: 'Fix authentication timeout bug'
Keywords: ['authentication', 'timeout', 'token', 'session', 'jwt']

Output: [
  'cass search "authentication timeout" --days 30',
  'cass search "token refresh" --days 60',
  'cass search "session expiry" --agent claude',
  'cass search "jwt validation error" --days 90'
]

QUERY GENERATION STRATEGY:
1. Use top 2-3 keyword combinations from keywords array
2. Add task-derived queries (extract noun phrases)
3. Vary --days parameter (30, 60, 90) for different time scopes
4. Include --agent filter if config.preferredAgent is set
5. Prioritize error/problem-oriented queries ('error', 'fix', 'bug')

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Escape quotes in query strings
- Limit to 5 suggestions (avoid overwhelming)
- Include at least one broad query (first keyword only)
- Include at least one specific query (multi-keyword phrase)

USE CASE:
User receives context but wants to explore more:
'The context you provided is helpful, but I want to see more examples of token refresh handling.'
→ They can run: cass search "token refresh" --days 60

INTEGRATION:
These queries appear at the end of markdown output under '### Dig Deeper' section
Also included in JSON output for programmatic access

- 'Suggested Searches'

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bb9","title":"[Cass] findUnprocessedSessions - Get sessions not yet reflected on","description":"Implement findUnprocessedSessions() to get sessions not yet reflected on.

## Signature
```typescript
interface ProcessedLog {
  processedSessions: Set<string>;
  lastProcessedAt?: string;
}

async function findUnprocessedSessions(
  processedLog: ProcessedLog,
  options: { days?: number; agents?: string[] },
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<string[]>
```

## Implementation Flow
1. Call cassTimeline(days) to get recent sessions
2. Extract all session paths from timeline groups
3. Filter out paths already in processedLog.processedSessions
4. Optionally filter by agent names
5. Return unprocessed session paths

## Error Handling
- Return empty array if timeline fails
- Handle missing timeline gracefully
- Log filtering progress at debug level

## Parameters
- processedLog: Tracks which sessions have been reflected on
- options.days: How far back to look (default: 7)
- options.agents: Filter by agent names (e.g., ["claude-code"])

## Algorithm
```typescript
const timeline = await cassTimeline(options.days || 7);
const allSessions = timeline.groups.flatMap(g => 
  g.sessions.map(s => s.path)
);

let unprocessed = allSessions.filter(path => 
  \!processedLog.processedSessions.has(path)
);

if (options.agents) {
  unprocessed = unprocessed.filter(path => {
    const session = timeline.groups
      .flatMap(g => g.sessions)
      .find(s => s.path === path);
    return options.agents.includes(session?.agent);
  });
}

return unprocessed;
```

## Usage
Core function for reflect command:
```typescript
const processedLog = loadProcessedLog();
const unprocessed = await findUnprocessedSessions(
  processedLog, 
  { days: 7, agents: ["claude-code"] }
);

for (const sessionPath of unprocessed) {
  await reflectOnSession(sessionPath);
  processedLog.processedSessions.add(sessionPath);
}

saveProcessedLog(processedLog);
```","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:04.582356-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:01.561059-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-bbb","title":"[Utils] hashContent - Normalized content hashing for deduplication","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:48.046449-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:48.046449-05:00","comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-bbb","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function hashContent(content: string): string
```

## Implementation Details
- Normalize content:
  - Convert to lowercase
  - Collapse multiple whitespace to single space
  - Trim leading/trailing whitespace
- Create SHA-256 hash using crypto.createHash('sha256')
- Update hash with normalized content
- Return first 16 characters of hex digest

## Why This Matters
Critical for exact duplicate detection in curator. Normalization ensures "Test Content" and "test  content" produce same hash, catching duplicates that differ only in formatting. Used in deduplication pass before semantic similarity check.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Empty string - returns hash of empty string
- Only whitespace - normalized to empty, returns hash of empty
- Very long content - SHA-256 handles any size
- Unicode characters - ensure proper encoding (UTF-8)
- Newlines and tabs - collapsed to single space by normalization","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
